signals.idea:
Milestone M0 from docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md — render telemetry + baseline.

Goal: stand up dev-only render instrumentation and capture baseline render counts for the most important visual surfaces before any architectural refactor begins. Without this baseline, every subsequent milestone's claim "we reduced renders by X%" is unfalsifiable.

## Deliverables

1. `src/shared/dev/useRenderBudget.ts` — a React hook:
   - Signature: `useRenderBudget(name: string, budget: number)` → void.
   - Tracks renders per mount via a ref, increments on each render.
   - On exceeding budget within an interaction window (default: 1 second since last reset), fires `console.warn('[RenderBudget] <name> rendered <count> times (budget <budget>)')`.
   - Resets count after a 1-second idle period (no renders for 1s).
   - Entire hook body wrapped in `if (import.meta.env.DEV)` so production build tree-shakes it. The return is `void` so callers don't need to handle undefined.
   - Must be a no-op in production — verify by checking final bundle doesn't contain the string "RenderBudget".

2. `src/shared/dev/RenderTelemetryOverlay.tsx` — a floating dev-only widget:
   - Bottom-right corner, fixed position, z-index high enough to overlay everything.
   - Shows a list of instrumented component names + current render count + budget status (green if under, red if over).
   - Toggleable via a keybind (Ctrl+Shift+R is fine; whatever's not already bound).
   - Hidden by default; stored-open preference in localStorage.
   - Only mounted in dev (`if (import.meta.env.DEV)`).
   - Mount it in `src/app/App.tsx` or the root layout so it sees all render events app-wide.

3. Instrumentation — call `useRenderBudget(name, budget)` at the top of these components. Confirm names against the repo first (some names may differ from this list; grep if unsure, and halt if a component name can't be found):
   - `ImageLightbox` (in `src/domains/media-lightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx`) — budget 5.
   - `EditModePanel` (in `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/EditModePanel.tsx`) — budget 3.
   - `LightboxShell` (in `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx`) — budget 5.
   - `TimelineCanvas` (in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`) — budget 3.
   - The timeline clip/row component — grep for it (`ClipRow` was referenced in an earlier draft but does not exist in this repo; find the real component that renders a single clip/track row and instrument it). Budget 2.
   - `MediaGalleryItem` (in `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx`) — budget 3.
   - `TasksPane` (in `src/features/tasks/components/TasksPane/TasksPane.tsx`) — budget 5.
   - `ShotsPanelContent` (in `src/features/editor/components/ShotsPanelContent.tsx`, NOT `ShotsPane`) — budget 5.
   - `GenerationsPaneGallery` (in `src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/components/GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx`) — budget 5.
   - `PreviewPanel` (in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/PreviewPanel.tsx`) — budget 5.

   Budgets above are starting heuristics. Confirm each is reasonable by running the dev build after instrumentation and observing actual counts during normal interactions — adjust budgets UP to real-world + 1 if the observed count is higher than the heuristic (so M0 doesn't ship with perpetual warnings for baseline behavior).

4. `docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md` — a new doc capturing measured baseline counts. Structure:

   ```
   # State Management Refactor — Baseline Render Counts

   Captured on <date> via useRenderBudget telemetry on commit <sha>.

   ## Per-component baselines

   | Component | Mount | Lightbox open | Clip select | Pane toggle |
   |---|---|---|---|---|
   | ImageLightbox | N | N | - | - |
   | EditModePanel | N | N | - | - |
   | ...

   ## Interaction sequences

   ### Opening image lightbox in video editor
   1. <component> rendered X times
   2. ...

   ### Selecting a timeline clip
   ...
   ```

   Minimum 3 interactions captured per applicable component. Numbers recorded should be from a production-built dev server (not HMR-affected) to avoid noise.

## Repo conventions (from reigh-app/CLAUDE.md)

- Prefer editing existing files; don't create unnecessary new files. The two new files above (`useRenderBudget.ts`, `RenderTelemetryOverlay.tsx`) are justified; any additional files require explicit justification.
- No hardcoded colors — use Tailwind theme tokens (`bg-background`, `text-foreground`, etc.) in the overlay.
- Tests only where the hook/widget behavior needs unit coverage — don't over-test.
- Don't touch anything outside `src/shared/dev/`, `src/app/App.tsx`, `docs/`, and the specific files being instrumented.
- Don't modify any edge function, supabase migration, or worker-orchestrator code.

## Gate criteria (for the human reviewer)

- `npx tsc --noEmit` clean.
- `npm test` matches baseline pass count.
- Production build succeeds: `npm run build` — and the final bundle under `dist/` does not contain the string `RenderBudget` or `RenderTelemetryOverlay` (grep the built assets).
- Baseline doc committed with at minimum 5 components × 3 interactions measured.
- Overlay toggle works in dev build; warnings fire when a synthetic burst exceeds a budget.

## Branch

`megaplan/m0-render-telemetry` off `main`. One commit or a small series. PR opened on push.

## Out of scope

- Do not refactor any consumer to reduce renders. This milestone is observation only.
- Do not install Zustand or introduce any store. That's M1.
- Do not touch `ImageLightbox`'s component composition beyond inserting `useRenderBudget` at the top.
- Do not fix any rendering bugs discovered during instrumentation — note them in the baseline doc as observations.

unresolved_flags.concern: Render telemetry baseline: plan requires measuring on a production preview build even though telemetry is dev-gated, so baseline capture would have no signal unless measurement uses a development-mode build artifact (for example `npm run build:dev` + preview).
Shortcut routing: `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R` is already consumed by AgentChat recording toggle, so reusing it for telemetry overlay introduces a direct user-input conflict.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The plan’s measurement flow conflicts with the requirement that telemetry is dev-only: Step 5 and Step 6 say to capture counts on a production preview build, but `useRenderBudget` and the overlay are specified to run only under `import.meta.env.DEV`, so production mode cannot provide those counts.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The baseline coverage requirement is under-specified in the plan text: Step 5.5 says 'at least five components with at least three measured interactions total', while the gate requires at least 5 components x 3 interactions measured, which is a stricter bar.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The idea references `docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md`, but this repository currently has no file at that path; the plan should explicitly note this missing source-of-truth doc and proceed using the supplied milestone brief as the authoritative requirement set.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R` is already bound in `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` to toggle voice recording, so using the same shortcut for the telemetry overlay would introduce a direct runtime keybind collision.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The production-bundle grep criterion as written is brittle in this repo because Vite build config enables `sourcemap: true` (`config/vite/vite.config.ts`), so source-map artifacts in `dist/` can still contain `RenderBudget`/`RenderTelemetryOverlay` strings even when production JS behavior is correctly tree-shaken.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The plan says the 'entire hook body' is wrapped by `if (import.meta.env.DEV)`; if implemented literally with hook calls inside that condition, it would violate normal React hook rules and existing repo pattern (`useRenderLogger`) where hooks are declared unconditionally and only runtime logic is gated.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Render diagnostics already exist in two systems (`src/shared/lib/debug/debugRendering.ts` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePerfDiagnostics.ts`) and several target components already call them, so introducing a third telemetry path without an explicit coexistence decision risks duplicated warnings/noise and unclear ownership of render instrumentation going forward.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The timeline 'single row' owner is broader than the initial list: the per-row render surface is `SortableRow` inside `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TrackListRenderer.tsx`, not `TimelineCanvas` itself, so the plan should lock this file as an explicit instrumentation scope to avoid stopping mid-execution.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: A required instrumentation location is currently only described abstractly ('verified single clip/track-row component') but not pinned to a concrete file path; repository inspection shows this should be `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TrackListRenderer.tsx` (`SortableRow`), and leaving it unresolved can cause incomplete coverage.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Several planned callers are high-cardinality lists (`MediaGalleryItem`, timeline rows), so a `useRenderBudget(name, budget)` API with only a shared name can only aggregate by component label unless instance IDs are added; the plan notes this as an assumption but does not convert it into an explicit product decision, leaving overlay semantics ambiguous.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: For wrapped components such as `TasksPane` and `PreviewPanel`, the executable caller is their inner implementation function (`TasksPaneComponent`, `PreviewPanelComponent`), so the plan should explicitly state that hook placement is in the inner render function rather than the memoized export wrapper.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 21.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 13.

items.criterion: `src/shared/dev/useRenderBudget.ts` exports `useRenderBudget(name: string, budget: number): void`, performs all telemetry work only under `import.meta.env.DEV`, resets after 1 second of idle time, and emits the specified warning when a budget is exceeded inside the active window.

`src/shared/dev/RenderTelemetryOverlay.tsx` is mounted only in dev, is hidden by default, persists open/closed state in `localStorage`, responds to a non-conflicting global keybind, and displays live instrumented component counts with under/over budget status.

All required surfaces are instrumented, including the verified timeline row component, and the committed budgets are calibrated to observed baseline behavior rather than left at values that warn during normal interactions.

`docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md` records the capture date, commit SHA, and measured baseline counts for at least five components across at least three applicable interactions, with narrative interaction-sequence notes for the key flows.
`npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` all succeed after the telemetry work is complete.
A grep of the built `dist/` assets finds no `RenderBudget` or `RenderTelemetryOverlay` strings.
The implementation stays within `src/shared/dev/`, `src/app/App.tsx`, `docs/`, and the explicitly instrumented component files, with no unrelated refactors or state-management changes.

The overlay uses existing Tailwind theme tokens instead of hardcoded colors, and the implementation avoids adding a new store/context or extra support files beyond the two approved dev files unless inspection proves they are required.
Manual preview-build smoke testing confirms persisted overlay visibility and at least one synthetic over-budget warning before the PR is opened.
